title: SOA-Harness v1.0 Audit Records Observability Response
description: Normative schema for the 200 OK response body of GET /audit/records (Core §10.5.3). Records are returned in chain order (earliest to latest). Consumers paginate via next_after until has_more is false, then verify the hash chain.

records.description: Audit records in chain order (earliest first). Three record shapes, discriminated on `decision`: (1) decision-audit rows (full §10.5 field set) when decision ∈ {AutoAllow, Prompt, Deny, CapabilityDenied, ConfigPrecedenceViolation}; (2) admin-audit rows (SubjectSuppression, SubjectExport, ResidencyCheck per L-41) with reduced required set; (3) SuspectDecision admin-rows per §10.6.5 (L-48) that additionally require referenced_audit_id pointing at the flagged original row. All shapes participate in the same hash-chain (prev_hash/this_hash).

subject_id.description: Stable identifier of the data subject implicated by this decision, or the literal string "none" when no personal data is touched. §10.7 authoritative.

tool.description: Tool name from the Tool Registry.

args_digest.description: sha256:<hex> for real invocations; SOA-PERM-RESOLVE-QUERY reserved for §10.3.1 synthetic queries (should NOT appear in real audit records since the endpoint is not-a-side-effect); GENESIS for the initial record marker.

signer_key_id.description: kid of the handler key that signed the PermissionDecision for Prompt decisions; empty string for decisions that don't require a signature (AutoAllow, Deny, CapabilityDenied).

billing_tag.description: OPTIONAL billing_tag from the Agent Card's tokenBudget.billingTag (§7 line 379) propagated to audit rows per §13.3 + §10.5. When present, MUST be part of the hash-chain content (covered by canonical JCS serialization of the record before computing this_hash). When absent, hash-chain computation excludes the field — impls MUST NOT emit billing_tag="" as a placeholder. Added in §19.4 errata 1.0.5 (L-40).

sink_timestamp.description: OPTIONAL external timestamp stamped by the WORM sink per §10.5.5 (L-48). Distinct from Runner-internal timestamp. Under normal operation |sink_timestamp − timestamp| ≤ 1s. Hash-chain participation semantics match billing_tag: when present, canonical-JCS-serialized into this_hash; when absent, excluded (not emitted as empty string).

retention_class.description: OPTIONAL per-record retention class per §10.5.6 (L-48). Derived from session's granted activeMode at append time: DangerFullAccess → dfa-365d (≥365d retention), others → standard-90d (≥90d). Immutable post-append (WORM).

referenced_audit_id.description: REQUIRED on admin-row subtype SuspectDecision per §10.6.5 (L-48). Points at the id of the original audit row being retroactively flagged as suspect due to handler-kid revocation within the 24h window.

next_after.description: id of the last record in this page. Omitted when records array is empty.

has_more.description: True when more records exist beyond this page. When false, next_after MAY be the current audit-log tail.

generated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the Runner computed this response. Under an injected test clock (§10.6.1 testability note) this reports the injected clock value.
